mentions.note: Defense contractor benefiting from Denmark NATO commitments (F-35 jets, military equipment); geopolitical stability dependent; tariff risks present but defense spending supports.

Bob asks about defense contractors with reasonable dividend and long-term appeal. I like RTX (aerospace/defense environment); liked since 1978. Trump's no-buyback/no-dividend threat makes hornet's nest for many.
Presidential social media pressure on buyback practices worked; company halted share repurchases to accelerate production and capacity; stock rallied nicely; dividend safe; clear message received.
HALO trade reversing; enterprise software catching bids; rotate out of defensive industrials.

Behind Patriot missiles (gold standard), SM3 interceptors, Tomahawk cruise missiles; government low on Tomahawks after recent Iran strikes; benefits from hot commercial aerospace market; up 58% last year; maybe best defense company on Earth.
Rare dip before quarter; buy it; dips tend not to last; mixture of defense and commercial aerospace resilience.

Strong Q1: Revenue +9-10% organic, op profit +14%, FCF +65%. Raised FY sales and EPS guidance. Pratt Whitney engines +23%, munitions +40%. Coyote counter-UAS ramping. Stock down 4.4% despite beat; attractive entry.
Aircraft maintenance/aerospace contractor hit hard by travel slowdown from Iran war; cutting guidance.
Solid beat, demand holding strong despite stock down 12% post-earnings—'silliest action of season.' Buy aggressively; aircraft servicing demand real; caller 6-for-6 on picks.
